mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, prompting risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in GDP growth (GDPC1) and industrial production (INDPRO) signal a contraction in economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or 60-day returns in contraction scenarios are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth (INDPRO) during early-cycle expansion reflects robust corporate earnings momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX fails to outperform baseline in 70%+ of instances where INDPRO growth exceeds 1.5% monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation expectations (CPIAUCSL) above 3.5% annualized pressure central banks to tighten policy, creating headwinds for equity valuations and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicates strong consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through earnings optimism.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or 20-day returns in expansion scenarios are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) above 150k signals a tightening labor market, boosting corporate profits and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX fails to outperform baseline in 70%+ of instances where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) during recessionary regimes indicates weak corporate activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances during recessions.
